analysis_method: Chrome CDP direct browser analysis on the target page. Captured rendered first-page DOM, one detail-page DOM, and a load-more click that triggered the real list API. No search engine and no non-browser target fetching used as primary evidence.

site.platform: Nuxt SSR list page with load-more fetch pagination and SSR-style /page detail template

notes: The first page is already present in rendered DOM on initial load.
Some list items do not have a thumbnail image, but the h3 link remains present.
Pagination is exposed as a load-more button that appends more rows into the same ul.l_list.
The first page did not trigger this list API during initial load because rows were already SSR-rendered in DOM.
A browser click on the load-more button triggered pageNo=2 and appended 20 more rows.
data.count was null in the captured response, so pagination termination should not rely on a total-count field.
The main article body is in the first child div under #bs_content.
Author and editor text appear later inside #bs_content but outside the main first-child content block.
The captured sample also contains media tags inside the article body.

spider_strategy.pagination: first page from DOM; later pages by replaying POST pageNo pagination via /open-service/content/getNewsIndexManyLevelByUuidSplit
notes: Use DOM selectors for the first page because rows are available immediately in rendered HTML.
For page >= 2, reuse the observed POST request with uuid and siteId fixed, incrementing pageNo.
Build detail URLs from article id plus siteLayoutModuleArticleStyleId when the API does not return a full URL field.
Keep list extraction scoped to ul.l_list to avoid right-rail side content.
